summary:
Fixed the Unicode BIDI privilege escalation vulnerability. The "needle" was the bug allowing attackers to register with usernames containing BIDI overrides (e.g., U+202E + "nimda") that visually render as "admin", tricking the auth system into granting admin access. 

Changes made:
- Modified `sanitize_username` in `sanitizer.py` to strip BIDI control characters (\u200E-\u200F\u202A-\u202E) before storing usernames.
- Updated `check_admin_access` in `auth.py` to compare against the stored username instead of the rendered display name.

Test.sh now passes, confirming the fix prevents the attack while maintaining normal functionality.
